contentBlocks.title: When do you need to convert Celsius to Fahrenheit?
contentBlocks.html:
Celsius (°C) is the temperature scale used by most countries worldwide. Fahrenheit (°F) is used primarily in the United States, its territories, and a few Caribbean nations. The conversion formula is: °F = °C × 9/5 + 32.

Common reference points: water freezes at 0°C (32°F) and boils at 100°C (212°F). Normal body temperature is 36.6°C (97.9°F). Room temperature is about 20–22°C (68–72°F).

This conversion is essential when traveling to the US, following American recipes (oven temperatures in °F), reading US weather forecasts, or dealing with medical temperature readings from US sources.

All calculations run locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server.

contentBlocks.title: How does Celsius to Fahrenheit conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
The formula is: °F = °C × 9/5 + 32. The reverse: °C = (°F − 32) × 5/9.

The scales intersect at −40°: −40°C = −40°F. This is the only temperature where both scales give the same number.

Quick mental math: double the Celsius value, subtract 10%, add 32. Example: 25°C → 50 − 5 + 32 = 77°F (exact: 77°F).

contentBlocks.title: Practical tips
contentBlocks.html:
• Water freezes: 0°C = 32°F. Water boils: 100°C = 212°F.
  • Room temperature: 20°C = 68°F. Comfortable range: 20–24°C (68–75°F).
  • Body temperature: 36.6°C = 97.9°F. Fever threshold: 38°C = 100.4°F.
  • Oven temperatures: 180°C = 356°F, 200°C = 392°F, 220°C = 428°F.

items.question: How do I convert Celsius to Fahrenheit?
What is 0°C in Fahrenheit?
items.answer: 0°C = 32°F. This is the freezing point of water.

items.question: What is 100°F in Celsius?
items.answer: 100°F = 37.78°C. This is slightly above normal body temperature.

items.question: At what temperature do Celsius and Fahrenheit match?
items.answer: At −40°: −40°C = −40°F. This is the only point where both scales give the same number.

items.question: What is a fever in Fahrenheit?
items.answer: A fever is typically defined as body temperature above 100.4°F (38°C).

items.question: Are calculations done locally?
items.answer: Yes. All calculations run in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
